sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 2.31 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.2)

statement.latex: Can every group admitting an ordering with only finitely many convex subgroups be represented by matrices over a field? { V.\,M.\,Kopytov} No, not every. For example, the group$G= < a_n,\,b_n\;(n ),\;c,\,d [a_n,b_n]=c , .$$. a_n^d=a_{n+1},\;\; b_n^d=b_{n+1}, \;\; [a_n,a_m]=[b_n,b_m]=[a_n,c]=[b_n,c]=1\; \;(n,m ) >$is soluble and orderable with finitely many convex subgroups, but it is not residuall...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
